article_title: China's NDRC Orders Meta to Unwind $2 Billion Manus Deal After Four-Month Investigation

content_preview.title: China's NDRC Orders Meta to Unwind $2 Billion Manus Deal After Four-Month Investigation
content_preview.summary: Beijing's National Development and Reform Commission ordered Meta and the AI agent startup Manus to undo their roughly $2 billion acquisition, capping a four-month review that began in January 2026 and citing concerns over the 'Singapore-washing' of a company with deep Chinese roots.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

China's National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC) on April 27 ordered Meta and the AI agent startup Manus to unwind a roughly $2 billion acquisition, [according to CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/27/meta-manus-china-blocks-acquisition-ai-startup.html). The order caps a four-month investigation that began in January 2026, when China's Ministry of Commerce announced it would assess whether the deal complied with the country's export control, technology import-and-export, an...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Round-1 findings cleanly addressed. Headline, summary, Overview, and What We Know all rebuilt around the verifiable timeline (Dec 30 2025 announcement → Jan 2026 MOFCOM assessment → April 27/28 NDRC unwind order). 'Singapore-washing' framing now correctly attributed to Fortune.

round_1_disposition.finding_1_reversal_narrative: Bot accepted finding. The 'reversal of prior approval' framing has been removed throughout — headline now leads with the four-month investigation, summary describes the review timeline, Overview replaces 'Beijing itself had cleared four months earlier' with 'four-month investigation that began in January 2026', and the What We Know section walks through the actual MOFCOM-to-NDRC sequence. The article now adopts Fortune's own 'Singapore-washing' framing, which is verified verbatim in the Fortune snapshot (3 mentions, including a direct attribution to a quoted analyst).
round_1_disposition.finding_2_wikipedia_attribution: Bot accepted finding. The previous 'incorporated in Beijing in 2022' has been replaced with 'founded in 2022 with operational offices in Beijing and Wuhan' — which matches Wikipedia's actual statement: 'The company maintained offices in Beijing and Wuhan'.

source_verification.https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/27/meta-man: Round-1 verification still applies. The MOFCOM January 2026 assessment language ('export controls, technology import and export, and overseas investment') and Xiao Hong/Ji Yichao exit ban verified.
source_verification.https://fortune.com/2026/04/28/china-blo: source-1.html — verified for: $2 billion deal value, 'complied fully with applicable law' Meta quote, investors 'shrugged off' framing, 'Singapore-washing' (3 mentions, including the analyst attribution and a hyperlink to a Fortune explainer at fortune.com/2026/03/09/singapore-washing-us-china-shein-manus/), Manus shutting down China operations.
source_verification.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manus_(AI_: source-2.html — verified for: 'company maintained offices in Beijing and Wuhan' (now correctly used by the article instead of the false 'incorporated in Beijing' claim), founded in 2022 by Xiao Hong, Singapore relocation, U.S. Treasury OISP review.
source_verification.https://www.aljazeera.com/economy/2025/1: Round-1 verification still applies. Meta's 'across our consumer and business products, including in Meta AI' quote, 80M virtual computers, $100M ARR all verified.

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified. The new framing aligns with what the sources actually say.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral. The Analysis paragraph appropriately uses Fortune's own framing rather than imposing a 'reversal' narrative the sources don't support.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. Both round-1 findings cleanly addressed. The rewrite is tighter than the round-1 version and correctly anchors the editorial framing (Singapore-washing, four-month investigation) to language and structure that's actually present in the cited sources.
